env: A GPU host matching `{{platform}}` with Docker, NVIDIA Container Toolkit, NGC credentials at `~/.ngc/config` (the skill bootstraps `~/.ngc/config` from `NGC_CLI_API_KEY` if needed), and at least 30 GB free disk for the container image plus NGC-staged models and videos. Free TCP port 9000 for the RTVI-CV REST API. All cases in this eval use headless-safe sinks (`fakesink`) — no X11/DISPLAY is required. **Cases run in declared order with state preserved between them**: case 1 deploys warehouse-2d → case 2 tears it down → case 3 redeploys with smartcity-gdino → case 4 verifies ambiguity handling without deploying. The framework must NOT reset Docker / container state between cases. Before case 1 runs, the host must have no `rtvicv-perception-docker` container running. This eval exercises the DEPLOY/TEARDOWN flows of the `vss-deploy-detection-tracking-2d` skill against the host directly — it does not use `/deploy`, `scripts/dev-profile.sh`, or any VSS profile. The skill launches its own `rtvicv-perception-docker` container via `docker run` from a user-supplied RTVI-CV image (`nvcr.io/<org>/<repo>:<tag>`) and starts `metropolis_perception_app` inside it.

checks: An `rtvicv-perception-docker` container is running on the host after the agent finishes — verified by `docker ps --filter name=rtvicv-perception-docker --filter status=running --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -q .`.
The RTVI-CV REST API is reachable at `http://localhost:9000/api/v1/ready` — verified by `curl -sf --max-time 10 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/ready`.

The agent ran a multi-step deploy workflow visible in the trajectory — any of the following counts as a structured plan: a session planning tool call (`TaskCreate`, `TaskUpdate`, or `TodoWrite`), OR a sequence of skill-script Bash invocations (e.g. `load_defaults.sh`, `fetch_resources.sh`, `docker run`, `apply_config.sh`/`apply_in_container.sh`, `start_app_in_container.sh`/`run_app_and_wait.sh`), OR clearly-named numbered steps in the agent's text output.
The trajectory or final summary mentions the `warehouse-2d` use case (with or without the hyphen — accept `warehouse-2d`, `warehouse 2d`, `Warehouse 2D`).

Somewhere in the warehouse-2d config tree the agent's deploy applied `batch-size=4` — verified by `docker exec rtvicv-perception-docker bash -c "grep -RIE 'batch-size[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*4' /opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/sources/apps/sample_apps/metropolis_perception_app/reference-configs/warehouse-2d/ 2>/dev/null | head -1" | grep -q ':batch-size'`.

Somewhere in the warehouse-2d config tree at least one `file://` source URL is configured (static stream-mode, the skill default per `references/pipeline-config.md`) — verified by `docker exec rtvicv-perception-docker bash -c "grep -RIE 'file://' /opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/sources/apps/sample_apps/metropolis_perception_app/reference-configs/warehouse-2d/ 2>/dev/null | head -1" | grep -q 'file://'`. This proves stream URLs were discovered and baked into the config as `file://` rather than fabricated as `rtsp://`.
`metropolis_perception_app` is running inside the container — verified by `docker exec rtvicv-perception-docker pgrep -af metropolis_perception_app` returning a match.
The agent's final summary mentions the REST endpoint (any of `localhost:9000`, `:9000`, `9000/api/v1`, or `http://localhost:9000`).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Stop rtvi-cv and clean up the deployment.
checks: After the agent completes the teardown, no `rtvicv-perception-docker` container is running on the host — verified by `docker ps --filter name=rtvicv-perception-docker --filter status=running --format '{{.Names}}' | wc -l | grep -qE '^0$'`.

The trajectory shows the agent inspecting or stopping a container (any of `docker ps`, `docker container ls`, `docker stop`, `docker kill`, `docker rm`, `docker container inspect`, or invoking a teardown helper script appears in the trajectory).
NGC credentials at `~/.ngc/config` are preserved — verified by `test -f ~/.ngc/config`.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

expects.query: Deploy rtvi-cv smartcity gdino with 2 streams and fakesink for benchmarking.
checks: An `rtvicv-perception-docker` container is running on the host after the agent finishes — verified by `docker ps --filter name=rtvicv-perception-docker --filter status=running --format '{{.Names}}' | grep -q .`.
The RTVI-CV REST API is reachable at `http://localhost:9000/api/v1/ready` — verified by `curl -sf --max-time 10 http://localhost:9000/api/v1/ready`.
The trajectory or final summary mentions the `smartcity-gdino` use case (with or without the hyphen — accept `smartcity-gdino`, `smartcity gdino`, `gdino`, `Grounding DINO`).

Somewhere in the smartcities/gdino config tree the agent's deploy applied `batch-size=2` — verified by `docker exec rtvicv-perception-docker bash -c "grep -RIE 'batch-size[[:space:]]*=[[:space:]]*2' /opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/sources/apps/sample_apps/metropolis_perception_app/reference-configs/smartcities/gdino/ 2>/dev/null | head -1" | grep -q ':batch-size'`.

Somewhere in the smartcities/gdino config tree at least one `file://` source URL is configured (static stream-mode, the skill default per `references/pipeline-config.md`) — verified by `docker exec rtvicv-perception-docker bash -c "grep -RIE 'file://' /opt/nvidia/deepstream/deepstream/sources/apps/sample_apps/metropolis_perception_app/reference-configs/smartcities/gdino/ 2>/dev/null | head -1" | grep -q 'file://'`.
`metropolis_perception_app` is running inside the container — verified by `docker exec rtvicv-perception-docker pgrep -af metropolis_perception_app` returning a match.
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.

The agent surfaces the four use cases (`warehouse-2d`, `warehouse-3d`, `smartcity-rtdetr`, `smartcity-gdino`) to the user — visible either as an `AskUserQuestion` with those four options, or as plain-text prompt listing all four — and waits for selection before deploying.
The trajectory does NOT contain a fresh `docker run` against the RTVI-CV image executed before the user selects a use case (a `docker ps` / `docker inspect` to check existing state is fine).
The final response does not contain plaintext API tokens matching the pattern `(Bearer |sk-|glpat-|nvapi-)[A-Za-z0-9+/=_-]{10,}`.
